metadata.summary: Two-track podcast strategy: guest appearances for faster ROI and hosting for long-term authority. Record 3+ episodes before launch to avoid schedule failures that damage algorithm placement.

content.title: Podcast Strategy
content.summary: Two-track podcast strategy: being a guest on others' shows (faster ROI) and hosting your own show (long-term authority asset). Guest appearances require no equipment investment and reach existing audiences; hosting builds a compounding owned asset. The core rule: record 3+ episodes before launching a hosted show — publishing day-one without a backlog leads to missed schedules that permanently damage algorithm placement.

why.p: Podcast listeners spend 30-60 minutes per session — no other medium delivers that depth of attention. Guest appearances on niche podcasts convert at 5-15 paid customers per appearance for SaaS products. Hosting builds consulting premiums and speaking opportunities over 12-24 months. Both paths compound: guest credits make hosting launches easier; a hosted show attracts higher-caliber guest pitches.

list.item: Drafting personalized podcast pitch emails for guest appearance outreach at scale.
Generating episode question sets from a guest's bio, past interviews, and show topic.
Writing episode show notes, chapter markers, and SEO-optimized descriptions.
Creating a media kit (bio, topic angles, previous appearances) for outreach campaigns.
Researching target podcasts (topic fit, audience size, booking frequency) from a keyword list.
Recording, editing, or audio post-production — requires human operation of audio software.
Live interview facilitation — depends on human listening and improvisation.
Deciding whether to host vs. guest-first without knowledge of current audience size.
Pitching to podcasts with 1M+ listeners as a first step — requires established credentials.
Estimating show growth without historical listener data.

section.name: guest-strategy
section.p: Finding relevant podcasts, pitching effectively, and converting appearances into leads and customers.

rule.statement: Default to guest appearances before hosting a show; guest strategy delivers faster ROI with zero equipment investment.
rule.rationale: Guest appearances reach existing audiences immediately. A single niche podcast appearance can produce 150+ trial signups and 30 paying customers. Hosting requires 12-24 months to deliver equivalent authority.

list.item: Listen Notes API: search by topic, filter by episode count and listener score.
Podmatch / MatchMaker.fm: guest-host matching platforms (manual workflow).
Competitor research: find where similar founders or experts appear.
Direct cold outreach to smaller niche shows (50K-500K listeners range).

rule.statement: Target podcasts with 50K-500K listeners in your niche; do not pitch 1M+ listener shows as a first step.
rule.rationale: Large shows require established credentials and relationships. Mid-tier niche shows have higher booking rates and more targeted audiences for B2B products.

section.name: pitch-rules

rule.statement: Reference one specific recent episode by name in every pitch; a pitch without a named episode signals templating and is ignored.
rule.rationale: Personalization is the primary differentiator; hosts receive dozens of generic pitches weekly.

rule.statement: Lead with audience value, not the guest's credentials; propose 3 specific bullet points the episode will cover.
rule.rationale: Hosts prioritize audience benefit over guest status; credential-first pitches convert at one-third the rate of audience-value pitches.

section.reference: Guest pitch email with personalization slots and 200-word constraint

antipattern.description: Generic pitch with "I'm a huge fan of your show" opener and no episode reference.
antipattern.reason: Signals mass templating; hosts delete immediately. 200-word personalized pitch with specific episode reference outperforms by 3-5x.

antipattern.description: Preparing only one topic angle for the pitch.
antipattern.reason: Hosts often prefer a different angle; offering 3 distinct angles increases booking rate significantly.

antipattern.description: No CTA during the appearance (no mention of where to find more).
antipattern.reason: Listeners who are not directed to a specific action take none; every appearance needs a clear verbal CTA.

example.title: SaaS guest strategy — 6-month outreach campaign
example.code: Approach: Pitched 30 niche podcasts, appeared on 15, shared unique framework each time Results: 2,500 website visits, 150 trial signups, 30 paying customers ($6K MRR), several partnership leads

section.name: hosting-strategy
section.p: Minimum viable equipment, episode structure, guest interview process, metrics, and agent workflows for hosting a show.

rule.statement: Record 3 episodes before publishing any of them; launching without a backlog causes schedule failures that permanently damage algorithm placement.
rule.rationale: Inconsistency is the single biggest cause of podcast failure. A 3-episode buffer ensures week-1 and week-2 can publish on schedule even if week-3 recording fails.

list.item: USB microphone: Audio-Technica ATR2100x ($50-100).
Recording: Riverside.fm (remote guests) or Audacity (solo).
Hosting: Transistor ($19/mo) or Buzzsprout — both have REST APIs.
Cover art: Canva (1400x1400px minimum).
Distribution: submit to Apple Podcasts, Spotify, Google Podcasts via hosting platform RSS.

section.name: episode-structure

rule.statement: Use a cold open (30-sec teaser from the episode) before the intro music; it hooks listeners before the generic intro can lose them.
rule.rationale: Podcast platforms show 30-second previews; a cold open converts at 2x the rate of a music-first intro.

section.reference: Reusable show intro with value proposition and episode hook slots.
Interview episode outline with question scaffolding and timestamps.

rule.statement: Research the last 5 interviews of every guest before preparing questions; generic biography openers waste the opening 5 minutes.
rule.rationale: Guests repeat the same origin story on every show; starting with insight-first questions produces more valuable content and higher completion rates.

list.item: Prepare 10 questions; use 5-7 in the actual interview.
Share general topics with guest, not exact questions — allows authentic responses.
Test tech 15 min before recording.
High-value question types: "Tell me about when you first realized...", "What's something most people get wrong about...", "What would you do differently if starting today?"
Downloads/episode first year: 100+ (good), 1,000+ (great).
Completion rate: 60% (good), 80%+ (great).
Guest booking rate: 30% of pitches (good), 50%+ (great).
Website referrals per episode: 50 (good), 200 (great).

rule.statement: Feed agent a transcript after recording to generate show notes, chapter timestamps, tweet thread, and newsletter blurb in one pass.
rule.rationale: Post-production content generation from transcript is the highest-leverage agentic workflow for podcasting; it converts 60 minutes of recording into 5 distribution assets.

example.title: Episode question generation prompt
example.code: Generate 10 interview questions for a podcast episode on [TOPIC]. Guest: [NAME], [ROLE], [COMPANY] Known for: [KEY INSIGHT OR ACCOMPLISHMENT] Show tone: [CONVERSATIONAL/TECHNICAL/STORYTELLING] Avoid: generic "what's your background" opener, "what advice would you give" closer. Include: one contrarian follow-up question per section.

section.name: tools-and-services
section.p: Tools for podcast discovery, recording, hosting, and distribution.

list.item: Listen Notes API: Search podcast database, get episode data. Install: pip install listennotes / listennotes.com/api
Podchaser API: Podcast and episode metadata, guest history. podchaser.com/api
Whisper: Transcribe recorded episodes locally. Install: pip install openai-whisper / github.com/openai/whisper
FFmpeg: Extract audio from video recording, trim silence. Install: brew install ffmpeg / ffmpeg.org
Transistor API: Publish episodes, manage RSS feed. developers.transistor.fm
Listen Notes: SaaS. REST API. Best podcast search API; free tier has 10 req/day.
Podmatch: SaaS. No API. Guest-host matching; manual workflow.
MatchMaker.fm: SaaS. No API. Similar to Podmatch; profile-based matching.
Transistor: SaaS. REST API. Hosting + RSS + analytics; full CRUD API.
Buzzsprout: SaaS. REST API. Hosting + distribution; slightly simpler than Transistor.
Riverside.fm: SaaS. Partial — Enterprise API. Best remote recording quality; no public API for free/pro.
Descript: SaaS. No public API. Transcript + editing; manual workflow.
Calendly: SaaS. REST API. Guest scheduling automation.
Opus Clip: SaaS. No public API. AI clip extraction for social; manual trigger.

section.name: best-practices
section.p: Guidelines for successful podcast marketing and guest appearances.

list.item: Send podcast pitches from a real email address the founder or host actively monitors — pitch replies need same-day response to maintain momentum.
Personalization means referencing a specific episode; "I love your show" without a named episode signals the email was templated.
Prepare 3 distinct topic angles per guest appearance, not one — hosts often prefer a different angle than the one you lead with.
Record the first 3 episodes before publishing anything; consistency in the first 30 days matters more than launch perfection.
Show notes should target a keyword the episode topic naturally ranks for — treat them as a blog post with an embedded audio player.
For guest strategy, track booking rate per outreach batch; below 20% means the pitch angle or target list needs revision.

section.name: agent-limitations
section.p: Where AI agents struggle with podcast workflows.

list.item: Podcast pitches generated without reading the actual show will contain generic phrases that signal templating — always provide show-specific context.
Episode questions from agents trend toward chronological biography ("Tell me about your journey"); explicitly prompt for insight-first, story-second structure.
Show notes generated from a transcript will over-summarize; they need editing to retain the guest's voice and quotable moments.
Agents cannot estimate a podcast's actual audience quality or conversion potential — download count is a proxy, not a guarantee.
Podcast scheduling logistics (calendar, Riverside link, reminder emails) should be automated via Calendly + webhook, not an LLM agent.

template.purpose: Guest pitch email template with personalization slots.
Interview episode outline with question scaffolding.
